content: If we model the active context of a reasoning LLM agent as a dynamic unified graph, where each token or reasoning step is a node, the LLM's working memory capacity limit directly acts as a hard constraint on zero-shot node generalization for new reasoning steps added to this context graph. LLM agents fail at long-range reasoning not just because they run out of context slots, but because out-of-distribution new reasoning nodes cannot be properly generalized and integrated into the working memory graph once working memory capacity is exhausted.
source_topics: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
Zero-shot node generalization on unified graph
trigger_topic: agent working memory capacity limits reasoning
